summary: Multiple posts describe Russian forces deploying small UAS in coordinated formations, including Mavic-type quadcopters arranged in rows of 3-5, and the use of specialized air defense drones like "Yolka" and "Elka" for kinetic intercepts of Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs. Ukrainian operators are employing FPV drones for direct attacks, including dual strikes on Russian infantry and attempts to disable enemy personnel before engagement. The "Rubikon" center reported 111 and 104 targets destroyed in two consecutive days, focusing on personnel and UAV control points across Donbas, Sumy, and Krasny Liman. Ukrainian forces are also increasing procurement of multirotor drones, with deliveries in January–February 2026 at least double the previous year’s pace.
